proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +41.1pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Regime stability is 'shifting' with only 1 day in Bear — if regime flips back to Choppy the cohort deactivates immediately, but no downside from approval itself
R-multiple 0.61 remains under D-8 review per project_anchor §7 (Day-6 forced-exit dominance) — keep monitoring
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +41.1pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification remains robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +41.1pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Current regime is Bear (day 1, shifting) so the cohort is now active, but Bear exposure is only 2/7 (28.6%) versus Choppy at 71.4%, below the 80% concentration trigger, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental risk. Consistent with continuous prior approvals (2026-04-29 through 2026-06-16) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type×regime×sector = UP_TRI × Choppy × Pharma (28.6%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type×regime×sector = UP_TRI × Choppy × Pharma; recommended action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI × sector=Pharma until the cell share drops below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits; do not add Pharma longs.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: If Choppy→Bear regime shift occurs while Pharma sector also rolls over, the 28.57% UP_TRI×Pharma cluster would unwind without statistical edge support, amplifying drawdown on a fully long book.

evidence: Matched cohort Choppy×UP_TRI at Candidate tier only (n=188) — unvalidated edge
Book is 100% directional long (UP_TRI + BULL_PROXY = 7/7), so a Choppy→Bear flip would unwind simultaneously
Choppy regime at 71.4% — concentrated but below 0.80 threshold
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in signal_type=UP_TRI × sector=Pharma until the cell share drops below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits; do not add Pharma longs.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: If Choppy→Bear regime shift occurs while Pharma sector also rolls over, the 28.57% UP_TRI×Pharma cluster would unwind without statistical edge support, amplifying drawdown on a fully long book.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: The UP_TRI×Choppy×Pharma cell breaches the 0.20 cell threshold at 28.57% (2 of 7 positions), and the matched Choppy×UP_TRI cohort sits only at Candidate tier (n=188) — unvalidated edge. While regime concentration (Choppy at 71.4%) and signal mix (UP_TRI at 71.4%) are within tolerance, the sector-clustered Pharma exposure in an unvalidated cohort is the actionable risk.
